title: Spain books a spot in the World Cup final with a comprehensive victory over France | CNN
fetched: raw-HTML 直抓 + 抽取(jsonld:articleBody) + 清洗（逐字、未经任何模型/改写/翻译）
original_text:
The semifinal that everyone predicted did not go the way than anyone expected. Most people, even Spain fans, thought France would dominate. If La Roja was to win, it would be from another late-goal or a mistake from the otherwise imperious French defense. The reality, though, was so very different. Spain started the match well and took charge of the ball, keeping possession but not exactly bothering the French goalkeeper much. Then, as the clock ticked past 20 minutes, Lamine Yamal’s effort was rewarded with a penalty to his team. The youngster quickly pressed France defender Lucas Digne, who was totally unaware of Yamal’s presence until it was too late.
As the defender swung his leg at the ball, Yamal nipped in ahead of him and was accidentally kicked by Digne. The referee pointed straight to the spot and there was no chance the video assistant referee (VAR) was going to overturn it. Up stepped Mikel Oyarzabal, who made no mistake from the penalty spot to score his fifth goal of the tournament. But France was not shocked into action after going behind. If anything, the goal only emboldened Spain further. Then, early in the second half, Pedro Porro became an unlikely hero after he bounced the ball off Dani Olmo. The Tottenham Hotspur defender placed his shot past the outstretched Mike Maignan in the France goal. Not even an hour had been played and Spain was deservedly 2-0 up.
The rest of the game was then just a bit strange. There didn’t seem to be an amazing atmosphere inside AT&T Stadium outside Dallas and France didn’t really offer anything to change that. Kylian Mbappé huffed and puffed a bit but never really threatened. The rest of his teammates just looked off the pace and seemed almost defeated at a time when they should have been throwing the kitchen sink at the Spanish defense. In truth, this was the first time the France team had really been tested this tournament. Safe to say, it was certainly not up to it today. It meant Spain reached its first World Cup final since 2010 and France is left looking to rebuild after what turned out to be Didier Deschamps last game in charge of the French national team. All eyes will now turn to Atlanta tomorrow as defending champion Argentina takes on England. A place in the final next to Spain is the prize.
